title: hieracium caesium subsp. pachyphyllum
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. pachyphyllum, also known as a mountain hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, the plant's aerial parts have been used in herbal medicine, showcasing its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hieracium caesium subsp. pachyphyllum include alleviating symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
